title: IT Projektmanager - Gesundheitswesen / Telematik (m/w/d)
required_skills: Project environment experience (sub-project leadership or IT project participation)
Understanding of project management methods (classic and/or agile)
German C1+ and good English skills
bonus_skills: Experience in IT projects in public sector, healthcare or infrastructure/rollout
matched_skills: Agile project management — sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives (Hapag-Lloyd)
Cross-functional stakeholder coordination across product, engineering and business teams (HSBC, Hapag-Lloyd)
Project documentation, reporting and status tracking (HSBC, ECE Group)
Risk identification and escalation (HSBC: risk escalation across 6 workstreams)
JIRA and Confluence proficiency — directly relevant IT project toolset
German B1 (meets minimum communication standard) and English C1
Structured working style demonstrated through budget dashboards and executive reports
core_responsibilities: Support overall project lead in large migration project in public healthcare (TI-Gateway)
Own sub-project responsibility including planning, coordination and implementation of rollout sites
Coordinate with clients, clinics and internal teams; support resource planning and project controlling
Monitor time, quality and budget; track project risks
Actively support rollout execution with flexible deployment
Progressively assume more responsibility towards independent project management
recommended_emphasis: Hapag-Lloyd: Agile sprint coordination as central interface between project teams, product owners and stakeholders — mirrors this role's coordination mandate
HSBC: Risk escalation and status reporting across 6 workstreams — demonstrates project controlling capability
JIRA and Confluence proficiency — core tools expected in this IT PM role
ECE Group: Project data tracking and progress reporting — directly relevant to controlling responsibilities
summary: Boru scores 55 (moderate match) driven by solid transferable PM coordination experience, agile methodology exposure, and JIRA/Confluence toolset. The role is entry-to-mid level in nature, seeking someone to grow into project leadership — well-suited to Boru's profile. The main gap is lack of healthcare or Telematikinfrastruktur domain knowledge, though the JD explicitly welcomes candidates without this background if they bring strong PM fundamentals. German B1 is the weakest point; C1 German is implicitly expected for daily client/clinic communication. Worth applying with emphasis on coordination and agile delivery breadth.
